info.title: Beta Features: Announcement Repository with TTS
info.description:
Features: Announcement Repository support reading and writing of Webex Calling Announcement Repository settings for a specific organization.

Viewing these read-only organization settings requires a full or read-only administrator or location administrator auth token with a scope of `spark-admin:telephony_config_read`.

Modifying these organization settings requires a full administrator auth token with a scope of `spark-admin:telephony_config_write`.

A partner administrator can retrieve or change settings in a customer's organization using the optional `orgId` query parameter.

400.description: Bad Request: The request was invalid or cannot be otherwise served. An accompanying error message will explain further.

401.description: Unauthorized: Authentication credentials were missing or incorrect.

403.description: Forbidden: The request is understood, but it has been refused or access is not allowed.

404.description: Not Found: The URI requested is invalid or the resource requested, such as a user, does not exist. Also returned when the requested format is not supported by the requested method.

405.description: Method Not Allowed: The request was made to a resource using an HTTP request method that is not supported.

409.description: Conflict: The request could not be processed because it conflicts with some established rule of the system. For example, a person may not be added to a room more than once.

410.description: Gone: The requested resource is no longer available.

415.description: Unsupported Media Type: The request was made to a resource without specifying a media type or used a media type that is not supported.

423.description: Locked: The requested resource is temporarily unavailable. A Retry-After header may be present that specifies how many seconds you need to wait before attempting the request again.

428.description: Precondition Required: File(s) cannot be scanned for malware and need to be force downloaded.

429.description: Too Many Requests: Too many requests have been sent in a given amount of time and the request has been rate limited. A Retry-After header should be present that specifies how many seconds you need to wait before a successful request can be made.

500.description: Internal Server Error: Something went wrong on the server. If the issue persists, feel free to contact the [Webex Developer Support team](/explore/support).

502.description: Bad Gateway: The server received an invalid response from an upstream server while processing the request. Try again later.

503.description: Service Unavailable: Server is overloaded with requests. Try again later.

504.description: Gateway Timeout: An upstream server failed to respond on time. If your query uses max parameter, please try to reduce it.

get.description:
Fetch a list of binary announcement greetings at an organization as well as location level.

An admin can upload a file at an organization level. This file will be uploaded to the announcement repository.

This API requires a full or read-only administrator or location administrator auth token with a scope of `spark-admin:telephony_config_read`.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: Get announcements in this organization.

parameters.name: locationId
parameters.description: Return the list of enterprise or Location announcement files. Without this parameter, the Enterprise level announcements are returned.

parameters.name: max
parameters.description: Limit the number of objects returned to this maximum count.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start at the zero-based offset in the list of matching objects.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Sort the list according to fileName or fileSize. The default sort will be in Ascending order.

parameters.name: fileName
parameters.description: Return the list of announcements with the given fileName.

parameters.name: fileType
parameters.description: Return the list of announcement files for this fileType.

parameters.name: mediaFileType
parameters.description: Return the list of announcement files for this mediaFileType.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Return the list of announcement files for this announcement label.

post.description:
Upload a binary file to the announcement repository at an organization level.

An admin can upload a file at an organization level. This file will be uploaded to the announcement repository.

Your request will need to be a JSON request with `application/json` Content-Type, including the announcement name, file URI, file name, and text-to-speech flag.

This API requires a full administrator auth token with a scope of `spark-admin:telephony_config_write` .

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: Create an announcement in this organization.

example.name: Public_Announcement
example.text: Welcome to our service. Please hold while we connect you.

put.description:
Modify an existing announcement greeting at a organization level.

An admin can upload a file or modify an existing file at an organization level. This file will be uploaded to the announcement repository.

Your request will need to be a JSON request with `application/json` Content-Type, including the announcement name, file URI, file name, and text-to-speech flag.

This API requires a full administrator auth token with a scope of `spark-admin:telephony_config_write`.

put.description:
Modify an existing announcement greeting at a location level.

An admin can upload a file or modify an existing file at a location level. This file will be uploaded to the announcement repository.

Your request will need to be a `multipart/form-data` request rather than JSON, using the `audio/wav` Content-Type.

This API requires a full administrator auth token with a scope of `spark-admin:telephony_config_write`.

parameters.name: locationId
parameters.description: Unique identifier of a location where an announcement is being created.

parameters.name: announcementId
parameters.description: Unique identifier of an announcement.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: Modify an announcement for location in this organization.

post.description:
Generate a text-to-speech prompt from the provided text, voice, and language.

Text-to-speech (TTS) efficiently generates prompts, greetings, and announcements by converting written text into synthesized audio using the specified voice. The generated audio functions like a recorded WAV file, eliminating the need for manual recording.

This API requires a full administrator or location administrator auth token with a scope of `spark-admin:telephony_config_write`.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: Generate text-to-speech for this organization.

example.text: Welcome to our service. Please hold while we connect you.

get.description:
Retrieve text-to-speech usage information, including the number of API calls made, the maximum allowed within the time window, and the timestamp indicating when the usage will reset.

Text-to-speech (TTS) efficiently generates prompts, greetings, and announcements by converting written text into synthesized audio using the specified voice. The generated audio functions like a recorded WAV file, eliminating the need for manual recording.

This API requires a full or read-only administrator or location administrator auth token with a scope of `spark-admin:telephony_config_read`.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: Get text-to-speech usage for this organization.

example.text: Welcome to our service. Please hold while we connect you.

get.description:
Get the status of a text-to-speech generation request by its ID. If the status is SUCCESS, the response includes `promptUrl`, `kmsKeyUri`, and `fileUri` to preview or use the audio prompt.

To preview the audio prompt:

1. Download the KMS key - use the Webex Node.js SDK and provide `kmsKeyUri` to download the key from KMS.

2. Download the encrypted audio - The encrypted audio file content is stored in cloud and can be retrieved using `promptURL`.

3. Decrypt the audio content - Use the jose library to decrypt the content downloaded from `promptUrl` using the downloaded key.

Text-to-speech (TTS) efficiently generates prompts, greetings, and announcements by converting written text into synthesized audio using the specified voice. The generated audio functions like a recorded WAV file, eliminating the need for manual recording.

This API requires a full or read-only administrator or location administrator auth token with a scope of `spark-admin:telephony_config_read`.

parameters.name: ttsId
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the text-to-speech generation request.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: Get text-to-speech status for this organization.

get.description:
Fetch a list of available text-to-speech voices. Use the returned voice ID in the generation request.

Text-to-speech (TTS) efficiently generates prompts, greetings, and announcements by converting written text into synthesized audio using the specified voice. The generated audio functions like a recorded WAV file, eliminating the need for manual recording.

This API requires a full or read-only administrator or location administrator auth token with a scope of `spark-admin:telephony_config_read`.

parameters.name: orgId
parameters.description: List text-to-speech voices supported for this organization.

isTextToSpeech.description: Indicates whether the announcement is text-to-speech.

fileName.description: File name of the uploaded binary announcement greeting.

fileSize.description: Size of the file in kilobytes.

mediaFileType.description: Media file type of the announcement file.

lastUpdated.description: Last updated timestamp (in UTC format) of the announcement.

featureReferenceCount.description: Reference count of the call features this announcement is assigned to.

featureReferences.description: Call features referenced by this announcement.

isTextToSpeech.description: Indicates whether the announcement is text-to-speech.

text.example: Welcome to our service. Please hold while we connect you.

fileName.description: File name of the uploaded binary announcement greeting.

fileSize.description: Size of the file in kilobytes.

mediaFileType.description: Media file type of the announcement file.

lastUpdated.description: Last updated timestamp (in UTC format) of the announcement.

featureReferenceCount.description: Reference count of the call features this announcement is assigned to.

featureReferences.description: Call features referenced by this announcement.

playlists.description: List of playlist available for selection.

isTextToSpeech.description: Indicates whether the announcement is text-to-speech.

text.example: Welcome to our service. Please hold while we connect you.

fileName.description: File name of the uploaded binary announcement greeting.

fileSize.description: Size of the file in kilobytes.

mediaFileType.description: Media file type of the announcement file.

lastUpdated.description: LastUpdated timestamp (in UTC format) of the announcement.

level.description: The level at which this announcement exists.

location.description: The details of location at which this announcement exists.

isTextToSpeech.description: Indicates whether the announcement is text-to-speech.

id.description: Unique identifier of the call feature referenced. The call Feature can be Auto Attendant, Call Queue or Music On hold.

name.description: Name of the call feature referenced.

type.description: Resource Type of the call feature.

locationName.description: Location name of the announcement file.

voice.description: The voice ID used to generate the audio prompt. Use the List Text-to-Speech Voices API to retrieve available voices.

text.description: The text to convert to speech.
text.example: Welcome to our service. Please hold while we connect you.

languageCode.description: The language code used to generate the audio prompt. Use the Read the List of Announcement Languages API to retrieve supported language codes.

GenerateTtsRequest.description: Request payload for generating a text-to-speech audio prompt.

id.description: Unique identifier of the text-to-speech generation request. Use this ID to track status using the Get Text-to-Speech Generation Status API.

GenerateTtsResponse.description: Response containing the ID of the text-to-speech generation request.

noOfApiCalls.description: The number of text-to-speech API calls made in the current time window.

maxAllowedApiCalls.description: The maximum number of text-to-speech API calls allowed in the current time window.

usageResetTimestamp.description: The timestamp when the usage counter will reset. It will be returned when reaching the maximum allowed API calls in the time window.

id.description: Unique identifier of the text-to-speech generation request.

voice.description: The voice ID used to generate the audio prompt.

text.description: The input text used to generate the audio prompt.
text.example: Welcome to our service. Please hold while we connect you.

languageCode.description: The language code used to generate the audio prompt.

status.description: The status of the text-to-speech generation request.

promptUrl.description: A URL to download the encrypted audio prompt. Only available when status is `SUCCESS`.

kmsKeyUri.description: The KMS key URI required to decrypt the prompt downloaded from `promptUrl`. Only available when status is `SUCCESS`.

fileUri.description: A file URI you can use when configuring an announcement. Only available when status is `SUCCESS`.

errorMessage.description: A detailed message describing why generation failed. Only present when status is `FAILURE`.

example.text: Welcome to our service. Please hold while we connect you.

TtsStatusResponse.description: Response containing text-to-speech generation status and file references.

TtsVoicesResponse.description: Response containing the list of supported text-to-speech voices.

id.description: The voice ID used to generate the audio prompt.

label.description: The voice label, including the voice name and gender.

id.description: A unique identifier for the playlist.
